rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 8, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. The declaration of Mr. Yan demonstrates that: (1) defendant Eng entered into four promissory notes and received money under those notes; (2) Plaintiff is the assignee of the lenders; and (3) the debts are owing and have yet to be repaid. In opposition, defendant Eng fails to present any competent evidence to create a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff to prepare a form of judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MARTIN DEL CAMPO VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION(SEIU) LOCAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 8, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The demurrer is overruled to the first cause of action because "it is reasonable that an investigation of the allegations in the original DFEH complaint would lead to the investigation of subsequent discriminatory acts," including discrimination based on the fact Plaintiff's disability required him to use medical leave. (Baker v. Children's Hospital Medical Center (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1057, 1065.) The demurrer is overruled as to the second and third causes of action because "[a] demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action." (PH II, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MARTIN DEL CAMPO VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION(SEIU) LOCAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 8, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION'S Motion To Strike. Granted. The words "The Family and Medical Leave Act (42 U.S.C. sec. 12101 et seq." are stricken from the caption page of the complaint. Plaintiff is not required to file an amended complaint to remove this language. Rather, this Order establishes that the reference to the FMLA is stricken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DAVID R. MUSTELIER VS. RADIANCE OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday July 8, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT RADIANCE OWNERS ASSOCIATION, MAYNARD/RICH MANAGEMENT COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA, CHRISTOPHER LECLERC, MATTHEW ECKMAN, MARGARET FAHNESTOCK, RICHARD DICKERSON, NANCY MADDEN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is denied. Sanctions in the amount of $2,010 are awarded against Defendants and shall be paid within 15 court days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TROIANO VS. SPLUNK INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Judicial Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 8, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT SPLUNK INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Judicial Proceedings. Granted. Plaintiff and Defendant entered into a binding arbitration agreement that provides that Plaintiff is required to arbitrate the causes of action in the complaint. (Declaration of Evangelina Vallez, Ex. A, Employment Agreement, par. 8.) Plaintiff's statements that he was required to sign the arbitration agreement as a condition of his employment and was not given an opportunity to negotiate its terms are insufficient to render the agreement procedurally unconscionable. (See Trend Homes, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 950, 958; see also Lagatree v. Luce, Forward, Hamilton and Scripps (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 1105, 1127.) Additionally, failure to provide the AAA Rules, without more, is insufficient to render the agreement procedurally unconscionable. (See Peng v. First Republic Bank (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1470.) Because Plaintiff has failed to establish procedural unconscionability, the agreement is enforceable. (See Fitz v. NCR Corp. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 702, 714, "Both procedural and substantive elements of unconscionability must be present for a court to refuse to enforce an arbitration agreement.") The litigation is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration. Plaintiff may adjudicate his claims for equitable relief in court once the litigation stay is lifted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
